heading: Order of special forfeiture
label: 3681 Order of special forfeiture
notes: Amendments1986—Subsec. (a). , struck out “chapter 227 or 231 of” after “restitution under”.Pub. L. 99–646, § 40inserted “an offense under or for”.Pub. L. 99–399section 794 of this title
Effective DateChapter effective 30 days after , see , set out as a note under , Crime Control and Law Enforcement.Oct. 12, 1984section 1409(a) of Pub. L. 98–473section 20101 of Title 34
amendments: Amendments1986—Subsec. (a). , struck out “chapter 227 or 231 of” after “restitution under”.Pub. L. 99–646, § 40inserted “an offense under or for”.Pub. L. 99–399section 794 of this title

children.label: a
children.text: Upon the motion of the United States attorney made at any time after conviction of a defendant for an offense under or for an offense against the United States resulting in physical harm to an individual, and after notice to any interested party, the court shall, if the court determines that the interest of justice or an order of restitution under this title so requires, order such defendant to forfeit all or any part of proceeds received or to be received by that defendant, or a transferee of that defendant, from a contract relating to a depiction of such crime in a movie, book, newspaper, magazine, radio or television production, or live entertainment of any kind, or an expression of that defendant’s thoughts, opinions, or emotions regarding such crime.section 794 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: An order issued under subsection (a) of this section shall require that the person with whom the defendant contracts pay to the Attorney General any proceeds due the defendant under such contract.

children.label: i
children.text: a money judgment rendered by a United States district court in favor of a victim of an offense for which such defendant has been convicted, or a legal representative of such victim; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a fine imposed by a court of the United States; and

children.label: i
children.text: satisfy a money judgment rendered in any court in favor of a victim of any offense for which such defendant has been convicted, or a legal representative of such victim; and

children.label: ii
children.text: pay for legal representation of the defendant in matters arising from the offense for which such defendant has been convicted, but no more than 20 percent of the total proceeds may be so used.

The court shall direct the disposition of all such proceeds in the possession of the Attorney General at the end of such five years and may require that all or any part of such proceeds be released from escrow and paid into the Crime Victims Fund in the Treasury.

children.label: d
children.text: As used in this section, the term “interested party” includes the defendant and any transferee of proceeds due the defendant under the contract, the person with whom the defendant has contracted, and any person physically harmed as a result of the offense for which the defendant has been convicted.
